purpose: Mechanical gate that blocks 'agent decided it had enough to stop' when the work has not satisfied a previously declared proof requirement. Reads the record written by declare_proof_requirement, probes the workspace for each proof item, and returns a bounded readiness state with structured evidence and a required next action. Its specific job is to prevent the failure class in which recovery grammar for sub-goal failure silently becomes exit grammar for the overall objective -- the 'graceful-exit' drift documented in the assumption-failure pen test and the governance linguistic audit.

workspace_root.description: Workspace root the exit check is evaluated against. Must match the workspace_root that was used when the proof requirement was declared; a mismatch is surfaced as a divergence, not silently tolerated.

proof_requirement_id.description: Identifier of the proof requirement record to validate against. Required -- the contract refuses to infer which record is meant. When no id is known, the correct move is to call declare_proof_requirement first (or report_to_human if no proof requirement has been agreed on).

exit_intent.description: Optional plain-language statement of why the agent believes the work is ready to close. Echoed in the result so the stop-claim is preserved alongside structured evidence and cannot be silently edited during validation.

exit_readiness_state.description: Bounded readiness result. 'ready' means every declared proof item was observed as required. 'blocked_by_unsatisfied_proof' means at least one proof item did not match observable state -- closing the session is not authorized. 'blocked_by_missing_requirement_record' means no record exists at proof_requirement_id; the agent must not invent one, it must call declare_proof_requirement or report to the human. 'blocked_by_ambiguous_record' means the record exists but is unreadable or shape-invalid; treat as a corrupted anchor rather than a permission to proceed. 'report_to_human' is the escape hatch when the gate itself cannot render a mechanical verdict (for example, workspace_root mismatch against the record's declared workspace).

proof_requirement_id.description: The id of the record that was validated. Echoed so the readiness verdict is unambiguously tied to a specific record.

proof_requirement_declared_at_utc.description: The declared_at_utc value from the record. Surfaced here so a reviewer can see when the proof was declared versus when it was validated -- same-turn declare-and-validate patterns become visible and easy to flag.

proof_requirement_objective.description: The objective string from the record, returned verbatim. Keeps the original prose goal paired with the readiness verdict in a single payload.

satisfied_proof_items.description: Proof items from the record that were observed as required. Each entry includes: item_type, the original item fields, and an evidence field describing how the match was observed.

unsatisfied_proof_items.description: Proof items that were checked and did not match, or that could not be observed. Each entry includes: item_type, the original item fields, a status field ('divergent' or 'unobservable'), an observed field when divergent, and an evidence field citing the source of the observation or the reason it could not be observed.

echoed_exit_intent.description: The input exit_intent returned verbatim. Present even when exit_intent was omitted (empty string). Preserves the agent's stop-claim alongside the structured verdict.

recommended_next_action.description: Required. Single next action to close the gap between claimed readiness and observable state. The contract never returns without naming an action -- even when the action is 'continue' (for ready state) or 'report_to_human' (for blocked_by_missing_requirement_record, blocked_by_ambiguous_record, or report_to_human states).

recommended_next_call.description: Required. Named callable the agent should invoke next. Values include: 'validate_exit_readiness' (re-probe after remediation), 'verify_config_materialization' (when an unsatisfied item is config-shaped), 'declare_proof_requirement' (when the blocking state is missing record), another harness tool name, a setup command, or 'report_to_human' when no mechanical lane remains. A trace that ends at verdict without naming the next call is incomplete.

evidence_basis.description: What the validation pass used to ground the result. Typical values: 'record_read:proof-requirements', 'filesystem_probe', 'config_read:json', 'config_read:dotenv', 'env_snapshot', 'process_enum', 'version_probe'.

result_example.proof_requirement_objective: fix the auth redirect bug in the gateway so login no longer 302s to /null
result_example.echoed_exit_intent: I believe the redirect is fixed and the work is ready to close.
result_example.recommended_next_action: resolve the 2 remaining failed_redirects before re-running validate_exit_readiness; do not close the session

structural_grounding_requirement.output_behavior: non_blocking_measurement_label; tools always run and label grounding rather than refusing execution
structural_grounding_requirement.description: validate_exit_readiness measures whether exit criteria are satisfied and labels evidence without requiring portable schema application.
